overview.summary: Technical specification for the TaskFlow Pro platform covering API design, database schema, authentication, AI service interface, and deployment architecture
overview.problemStatement: Development teams need a clear, detailed technical blueprint to implement the TaskFlow Pro platform consistently across frontend, backend, ML, and infrastructure layers
overview.proposedSolution: A three-tier architecture with React SPA frontend, Fastify API backend, and Python ML microservice, deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with PostgreSQL and Redis

goals.goal: Define comprehensive API contracts for all TaskFlow Pro endpoints
goals.successCriteria: All endpoints documented with request/response schemas

goals.goal: Specify database schema supporting task management and AI prioritization
goals.successCriteria: Schema supports all CRUD operations and priority scoring workflows

goals.goal: Establish security architecture meeting enterprise standards
goals.successCriteria: JWT auth, encryption at rest and in transit, input validation

implementationPlan.overview: Phased implementation starting with core API and database, followed by AI service integration, then frontend and deployment infrastructure

phases.description: Implement authentication, task CRUD, and project management APIs with PostgreSQL schema
tasks: Set up Fastify server with TypeScript
Create database migrations for all tables
Build task and project CRUD endpoints
deliverables: PostgreSQL schema with all tables and indexes

phases.description: Build Python ML microservice for priority scoring and connect integration sync workers
tasks: Build BullMQ worker for async jobs

phases.description: Build React SPA and deploy all services to AWS ECS Fargate
deliverables: All services deployed on ECS Fargate

content.scope: Covers API design, database schema, authentication, AI service interface, and deployment architecture for the TaskFlow Pro platform

systemArchitecture.overview: Three-tier architecture with React SPA frontend, Fastify API backend, and Python ML microservice. PostgreSQL for persistence, Redis for caching/sessions, BullMQ for async jobs. All services containerized and deployed on AWS ECS Fargate.

services.name: taskflow-web
services.description: Single-page application serving the main UI including dashboard, task management, Focus Mode, and integration settings

services.name: taskflow-api
services.description: Core API handling authentication, task CRUD, project management, user settings, and orchestrating AI priority requests

services.name: taskflow-ml
services.deployment: ECS Fargate with GPU instances for training, CPU for inference
services.description: AI prioritization engine providing priority scoring via gRPC. Handles model inference, retraining, and explanation generation.

services.name: taskflow-worker
services.description: Processes async jobs including integration syncs, email notifications, analytics aggregation, and scheduled priority recalculations

apiDesign.authentication: JWT Bearer tokens with 15-minute expiry, refresh tokens stored in HttpOnly cookies

endpoints.description: Authenticate user with email/password or OAuth token
endpoints.requestBody: { email: string, password: string } | { provider: string, token: string }

endpoints.description: List tasks with filtering, sorting, and AI priority scores
endpoints.requestBody: Query params: projectId, status, priority, sort, limit, offset
endpoints.responseBody: { tasks: Task[], total: number, hasMore: boolean }

endpoints.requestBody: { title: string, description?: string, projectId: string, dueDate?: string, labels?: string[] }

endpoints.description: Request AI priority scoring for a set of tasks

apiDesign.errorFormat: { error: { code: string, message: string, details?: any } }
apiDesign.rateLimiting: 100 req/min authenticated, 20 req/min unauthenticated. 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header.

tables.name: users
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), email (UNIQUE), password_hash, display_name, avatar_url, created_at, updated_at, last_login_at, settings (JSONB)

tables.name: projects
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), name, description, owner_id (FK users), color, icon, created_at, updated_at, archived_at

tables.name: tasks
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), title, description, project_id (FK projects), creator_id (FK users), assignee_id (FK users), status (enum), due_date, priority_score (float), priority_explanation (text), labels (text[]), parent_task_id (FK tasks), position (int), created_at, updated_at, completed_at

tables.name: focus_sessions
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (FK users), started_at, ended_at, task_count, tasks_completed, duration_seconds, session_type (enum)

tables.name: integrations
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (FK users), provider (enum), access_token_encrypted, refresh_token_encrypted, config (JSONB), last_sync_at, sync_status (enum), created_at

tables.name: priority_feedback
tables.description: User feedback on AI priority suggestions
tables.columns: id (UUID PK), user_id (FK users), task_id (FK tasks), suggested_score (float), user_adjusted_score (float), feedback_type (enum), created_at

indexes: tasks_priority_score_idx ON tasks(priority_score DESC) WHERE status = 'active'

securityConsiderations.approach: JWT with short-lived access tokens (15 min) and long-lived refresh tokens (7 days). Refresh tokens stored in HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookies. Token rotation on every refresh.
securityConsiderations.mitigations: Short expiry, HttpOnly cookies, token rotation, IP-based anomaly detection

securityConsiderations.approach: TLS 1.3 in transit. AES-256 at rest for database. Integration tokens encrypted with per-user keys derived from KMS.
securityConsiderations.mitigations: Encryption at rest and in transit, key rotation, AWS KMS for key management

securityConsiderations.approach: Zod schemas for all API inputs. Strict Content-Type enforcement. HTML sanitization for user text fields. SQL injection prevented by Drizzle ORM parameterized queries.
securityConsiderations.mitigations: Schema validation, parameterized queries, CSP headers, input sanitization

performanceTargets.measurement: Core Web Vitals via CloudWatch RUM
